jito.selfDealingNotes: No token purchase required. MEV commission required but performance-based.
jito.transparencyNotes: Published criteria, public API, Jito Foundation governance
jito.mevPolicy: Jito client required for all validators. Tips redistributed to stakers minus commission. Validators set their own MEV commission (typically 5-8%).

doublezero.selfDealingNotes: Network membership fee structure, but no token flywheel
doublezero.transparencyNotes: Published criteria, some transparency around selection
doublezero.mevPolicy: No public MEV redistribution policy. Validators may run Jito client independently.

blazestake.transparencyNotes: Open governance, published criteria, community voting
blazestake.mevPolicy: Requires Jito client. MEV tips flow to stakers. Community governance via BlazeStake DAO.

jpool.selfDealingNotes: No token purchase required for delegation
jpool.mevPolicy: Encourages Jito client. MEV tips redistributed. Commission monitoring in place.

phase.selfDealingNotes: No token or self-delegation requirement. Simply prioritizes small validators.
phase.transparencyNotes: Published criteria, open application, clear mission
phase.mevPolicy: Jito client recommended. Focuses on small validators — MEV tips pass through where available.

vault.selfDealingNotes: Validators MUST buy $V tokens and stake through vSOL to qualify. Top 100 vSOL delegators share 40% of undirected stake. Classic token flywheel.
vault.transparencyNotes: Discord-only application, opaque board approval, Votex.so governance
vault.mevPolicy: No public MEV policy. Token flywheel prioritizes $V holders over staker MEV returns.

dynosol.selfDealingNotes: No token requirement. Reserves large portion of pool for new and small validators.
dynosol.transparencyNotes: Open validator application, monthly rebalancing, docs available at docs.dynosol.io
dynosol.mevPolicy: No formal MEV redistribution policy documented. Validators may run Jito independently.

jagpool.transparencyNotes: Published delegation strategy focused on geographic diversity
jagpool.mevPolicy: Jito client required. MEV tips flow to stakers. Geographic diversity focus.

stke.mevPolicy: Limited MEV policy documentation. Some validators run Jito.

marinade.selfDealingNotes: PSR auction model — validators bid for stake, which creates indirect self-dealing pressure
marinade.transparencyNotes: Open source delegation strategy, but PSR auction opaque to outsiders
marinade.mevPolicy: Jito client strongly preferred. PSR auction model means validators internalize MEV earnings into their bids. Tips flow to stakers through mSOL APY.

shinobi.mevPolicy: Jito client required. Community pool with MEV tips to stakers.

edgevana.selfDealingNotes: Edgevana infrastructure customers may receive preferential delegation
edgevana.transparencyNotes: Some published criteria, but selection tied to infrastructure business
edgevana.mevPolicy: Jito client available on Edgevana infrastructure. MEV policy tied to infrastructure offering.

definity.mevPolicy: Jito client encouraged. Geographic diversity focused delegation.

indiesol.selfDealingNotes: No token requirement, indie validator focused
indiesol.transparencyNotes: Small community pool, limited formal documentation
indiesol.mevPolicy: Small indie pool. Some validators run Jito independently. No formal MEV policy.
